title: Attack on Pearl Harbor takes place
subtitle: Battle in South-East Asian theater of World War II
fact: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

title: Battle of Waterloo takes place
subtitle: Battle in Waterloo Campaign
fact: The Battle of Waterloo was fought on Sunday 18 June 1815, near Waterloo, marking the end of the Napoleonic Wars.

title: Battle of Karbala takes place
subtitle: Battle in Second Fitna
fact: The Battle of Karbala was fought on 10 October 680 between the army of the second Umayyad caliph Yazid I and a small army led by Husayn ibn Ali, the grandson of the Islamic prophet Muhammad, at Karbala, Sawad.

title: Battle of Thermopylae takes place
subtitle: Battle in Second Persian invasion of Greece
fact: The Battle of Thermopylae was fought in 480 BC at Thermopylae between the Achaemenid Persian Empire under Xerxes I and an alliance of Greek city-states led by Sparta under Leonidas I.

title: Battle of Badr takes place
subtitle: Battle in Muslim–Quraysh War
fact: The Battle of Badr or sometimes called The Raid of Badr, also referred to as The Day of the Criterion in the Qur'an and by Muslims, was fought on 13 March 624 CE, near the present-day city of Badr, Al Madinah Province in Saudi Arabia.

title: Battle of Hastings takes place
subtitle: Battle in Norman conquest of England
fact: The Battle of Hastings was fought on 14 October 1066 between the Norman-French army of William, Duke of Normandy, and an English army under the Anglo-Saxon King Harold Godwinson, beginning the Norman Conquest of England.

title: Battle of Okinawa takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War II
fact: The Battle of Okinawa, codenamed Operation Iceberg, was a major battle of the Pacific War fought on the island of Okinawa by the United States Army and United States Marine Corps forces against the Imperial Japanese Army.

title: Dunkirk evacuation takes place
subtitle: Battle in Battle of France
fact: In the Dunkirk evacuation, codenamed Operation Dynamo and also known as the Miracle of Dunkirk, or just Dunkirk, more than 338,000 Allied soldiers were evacuated during the Second World War from the beaches and harbour of Dunkirk in northern France between 26 May and 4 June 1940.

title: Battle of Agincourt takes place
subtitle: Battle in Hundred Years' War
fact: The Battle of Agincourt was an English victory in the Hundred Years' War.

title: Battle of Vienna takes place
subtitle: Battle in Muslim conquests
fact: The Battle of Vienna took place at Kahlenberg Mountain near Vienna on 12 September 1683 after the city had been besieged by the Ottoman Empire for two months.

title: Battle of Trafalgar takes place
subtitle: Battle in Trafalgar Campaign
fact: The Battle of Trafalgar was a naval engagement that took place on 21 October 1805 between the Royal Navy and a combined fleet of the French and Spanish navies during the War of the Third Coalition.

title: Battle of Britain takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War II
fact: The Battle of Britain was a military campaign of the Second World War, in which the Royal Air Force (RAF) and the Fleet Air Arm (FAA) of the Royal Navy defended the United Kingdom against large-scale attacks by Nazi Germany's air force, the Luftwaffe.

title: Battle of Antietam takes place
subtitle: Battle in American Civil War
fact: The Battle of Antietam, also called the Battle of Sharpsburg, particularly in the Southern United States, took place during the American Civil War on September 17, 1862, between Confederate General Robert E.

title: Fall of Saigon takes place
subtitle: Battle in Vietnam War
fact: Saigon, the capital of South Vietnam, was captured by North Vietnam on 30 April 1975.

title: Battle of the Teutoburg Forest takes place
subtitle: Battle in Early Imperial campaigns in Germania
fact: The Battle of the Teutoburg Forest, also called the Varus Disaster or Varian Disaster by Roman historians, was a major battle fought between an alliance of Germanic peoples and the Roman Empire between 8 and 11 September 9 AD, possibly near modern Kalkriese.

title: Battle of Kursk takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War II
fact: The Battle of Kursk, also called the Battle of the Kursk Salient, was a major World War II Eastern Front battle between the forces of Nazi Germany and the Soviet Union near Kursk in southwestern Russia during the summer of 1943, resulting in a Soviet victory.

title: Battle of Austerlitz takes place
subtitle: Battle in War of the Third Coalition
fact: The Battle of Austerlitz, also known as the Battle of the Three Emperors, was one of the most important military engagements of the Napoleonic Wars.

title: Battle of Culloden takes place
subtitle: Battle in Jacobite risings
fact: The Battle of Culloden took place on 16 April 1746, near Inverness, in the Scottish Highlands.

title: Battle of Uhud takes place
subtitle: Battle in Muslim–Quraysh War
fact: The Battle of Uhud was fought between the early Muslims and the Quraysh during the Muslim–Quraysh wars in a valley north of Medina near Mount Uhud on Saturday, 23 March 625 AD.

title: Siege of Yorktown takes place
subtitle: Battle in American Revolutionary War
fact: The Siege of Yorktown, also known as the Battle of Yorktown and the surrender at Yorktown, was the final major land engagement of the American Revolutionary War.

title: Battle of Cannae takes place
subtitle: Battle in Second Punic War
fact: The Battle of Cannae was a key engagement of the Second Punic War between the Roman Republic and Carthage, fought on 2 August 216 BC near the ancient village of Cannae in Apulia, southeast Italy.

title: First Battle of Bull Run takes place
subtitle: Battle in Eastern theater of the American Civil War
fact: The First Battle of Bull Run, called the Battle of First Manassas by Confederate forces, was the first major battle of the American Civil War.

title: Battle of Plassey takes place
subtitle: Battle
fact: The Battle of Plassey was a decisive victory of the British East India Company, under the leadership of Robert Clive, over the Nawab of Bengal and his French allies on 23 June 1757.

title: Battles of Lexington and Concord takes place
subtitle: Battle in American Revolutionary War
fact: The Battles of Lexington and Concord on April 19, 1775, were the first major military actions between the British Army and Patriot militias from British America's Thirteen Colonies during the American Revolutionary War.

title: Battle of Khaybar takes place
subtitle: Battle in battles of Muhammad
fact: The Battle of Khaybar was an armed confrontation between the early Muslims and the Jewish community of Khaybar in 628 CE.

title: Battle of Sekigahara takes place
subtitle: Battle in Sekigahara Campaign
fact: The Battle of Sekigahara was an important battle in Japan which occurred on October 21, 1600 in what is now Gifu Prefecture, Japan, at the end of the Sengoku period.

title: Battle of the Camel takes place
subtitle: Battle in First Fitna
fact: The Battle of the Camel, also known as the Battle of Basra took place in 656 CE outside of Basra in Iraq.

title: Battle of Bosworth Field takes place
subtitle: Battle in Wars of the Roses
fact: The Battle of Bosworth or Bosworth Field was the last significant battle of the Wars of the Roses, the civil war between the houses of Lancaster and York that extended across England and Wales in the latter half of the 15th century.

title: Battle of Marathon takes place
subtitle: Battle in First Persian invasion of Greece
fact: The Battle of Marathon took place in 490 BC during the first Persian invasion of Greece.

title: Battle of Stamford Bridge takes place
subtitle: Battle in Viking activity in the British Isles
fact: The Battle of Stamford Bridge took place at the village of Stamford Bridge, East Riding of Yorkshire, in England, on 25 September 1066, between an English army under King Harold Godwinson and an invading Norwegian force led by King Harald Hardrada and the English king's brother Tostig Godwinson.

title: Battle of Adwa takes place
subtitle: Battle in First Italo-Ethiopian War
fact: The Battle of Adwa was the climactic battle of the First Italo-Ethiopian War.

title: Battle of Bunker Hill takes place
subtitle: Battle in American Revolutionary War
fact: The Battle of Bunker Hill was fought on June 17, 1775, during the Siege of Boston in the first stage of the American Revolutionary War.

title: Battles of Saratoga takes place
subtitle: Battle in American Revolutionary War
fact: The Battles of Saratoga were fought September 19 and October 7, 1777 over the same ground by the American Continental Army and the British Army near Saratoga, New York during the American Revolutionary War.

title: Battle of Carrhae takes place
subtitle: Battle in Crassus' Parthian campaign
fact: The Battle of Carrhae was fought in 53 BC between the Roman Republic and the Parthian Empire near the ancient town of Carrhae.

title: Battle of Moscow takes place
subtitle: Battle in Eastern Front
fact: The Battle of Moscow was a military campaign that consisted of two periods of strategically significant fighting on a 600 km (370 mi) sector of the Eastern Front during World War II, between October 1941 and January 1942.

title: Battle of Isandlwana takes place
subtitle: Battle in Anglo-Zulu War
fact: The Battle of Isandlwana on 22 January 1879 was the second major encounter in the Anglo-Zulu War between the British Empire and the Zulu Kingdom – the Battle of Nyezane having been fought and won earlier on the same day by Colonel Pearson's Coastal Column.

title: Battle of Borodino takes place
subtitle: Battle in French invasion of Russia
fact: The Battle of Borodino or the Battle of Moscow took place on the outskirts of Moscow near the village of Borodino on 7 September 1812, during Napoleon's invasion of Russia.

title: Battle of Peleliu takes place
subtitle: Battle in Pacific War
fact: The Battle of Peleliu, codenamed Operation Stalemate II by the US military, was fought between the United States and Japan during the Mariana and Palau Islands campaign of World War II, from 15 September to 27 November 1944, on the island of Peleliu.

title: Battle of Tours takes place
subtitle: Battle in Umayyad invasion of Gaul
fact: The Battle of Tours, also called the Battle of Poitiers and the Battle of the Highway of the Martyrs, was fought on 10 October 732, and was an important battle during the Umayyad invasion of Gaul.

title: Battle of Manzikert takes place
subtitle: Battle in Byzantine–Seljuq Wars
fact: The Battle of Manzikert or Malazgirt was fought between the Byzantine Empire and the Seljuk Empire on 26 August 1071 near Manzikert, Iberia.

title: Battle of Castle Itter takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War II
fact: The Battle of Castle Itter was fought on 5 May 1945, in the Austrian village of Itter in the North Tyrol region of the country, during the last days of the European Theater of World War II.

title: Battle of Salamis takes place
subtitle: Battle in Second Persian invasion of Greece
fact: The Battle of Salamis was a naval battle fought in 480 BC, between an alliance of Greek city-states under Themistocles, and the Achaemenid Empire under King Xerxes.

title: Battle of Gaugamela takes place
subtitle: Battle in Wars of Alexander the Great
fact: The Battle of Gaugamela, also called the Battle of Arbela, took place in 331 BC between the forces of the Army of Macedon under Alexander the Great and the Persian Army under King Darius III.

title: Third Battle of Panipat takes place
subtitle: Battle
fact: The Third Battle of Panipat took place on 14 January 1761 between the Maratha Empire and the invading army of the Durrani Empire.

title: Dieppe Raid takes place
subtitle: Battle in North West Europe Campaign
fact: Operation Jubilee or the Dieppe Raid was an Allied amphibious attack on the German-occupied port of Dieppe in northern France, during the Second World War.

title: Battle of Nicopolis takes place
subtitle: Battle in Ottoman Wars in Europe
fact: The Battle of Nicopolis took place on 25 September 1396 and resulted in the rout of an allied Crusader army, assisted by the Venetian navy, at the hands of an Ottoman force, raising the siege of the Danubian fortress of Nicopolis and leading to the end of the Second Bulgarian Empire.

title: Battle of Crécy takes place
subtitle: Battle in Hundred Years' War
fact: The Battle of Crécy took place on 26 August 1346 in northern France between a French army commanded by King Philip VI and an English army led by King Edward III.

title: Battle of Fort Sumter takes place
subtitle: Battle in American Civil War
fact: The Battle of Fort Sumter was the bombardment of Fort Sumter near Charleston, South Carolina, by the South Carolina militia.

title: Battle of the Trench takes place
subtitle: Battle in Muslim–Quraysh War
fact: The Battle of the Trench, also known as the Battle of Khandaq and the Battle of the Confederates, was part of the conflict between the Muslims and the Quraysh.

title: Battle of San Jacinto takes place
subtitle: Battle in Texas Revolution
fact: The Battle of San Jacinto, fought on April 21, 1836, in present-day La Porte and Deer Park, Texas, was the final and decisive battle of the Texas Revolution.

title: Battle of Towton takes place
subtitle: Battle in Wars of the Roses
fact: The Battle of Towton took place on 29 March 1461 during the Wars of the Roses, near Towton in North Yorkshire.

title: Battle of Kosovo takes place
subtitle: Battle in Ottoman Wars in Europe
fact: The Battle of Kosovo took place on 15 June 1389 between an army led by the Serbian Prince Lazar Hrebeljanović and an invading army of the Ottoman Empire under the command of Sultan Murad I.

title: Battle of Actium takes place
subtitle: Battle in Final War of the Roman Republic
fact: The Battle of Actium was a naval battle fought between Octavian's maritime fleet, led by Marcus Agrippa, and the combined fleets of Mark Antony and Cleopatra.

title: Battle of the Boyne takes place
subtitle: Battle in Williamite War in Ireland
fact: The Battle of the Boyne took place in 1690 between the forces of the deposed King James II, and those of King William III who, with his wife Queen Mary II, had acceded to the Crowns of England and Scotland in 1689.

title: Battle of Mu'tah takes place
subtitle: Battle in Arab–Byzantine Wars
fact: The Battle of Mu'tah took place in September 629, between the forces of Muhammad and the army of the Byzantine Empire and their Ghassanid vassals.

title: Battle of Grunwald takes place
subtitle: Battle in Polish-Lithuanian-Teutonic War
fact: The Battle of Grunwald was fought on 15 July 1410 during the Polish–Lithuanian–Teutonic War.

title: Battle of Mohács takes place
subtitle: Battle in Hungarian–Ottoman War
fact: The Battle of Mohács took place on 29 August 1526 near Mohács, in the Kingdom of Hungary.

title: Battle of Plataea takes place
subtitle: Battle in Greco-Persian Wars
fact: The Battle of Plataea was the final land battle during the second Persian invasion of Greece.

title: Battle of Buxar takes place
subtitle: Battle in Bengal War
fact: The Battle of Buxar was fought between 22 and 23 October 1764, between the forces of the British East India Company, under the command of Major Hector Munro, against the combined armies of Shah Alam II, the Emperor of the Mughal Empire; Mir Qasim, the Nawab of Bengal; Balwant Singh, the Maharaja of the Benares State; Shuja-ud-daula, the Nawab of Awadh.

title: Battle of Zama takes place
subtitle: Battle in Second Punic War
fact: The Battle of Zama was fought in 202 BC in what is now Tunisia between a Roman army commanded by Scipio Africanus and a Carthaginian army commanded by Hannibal.

title: Battle of Kasserine Passe takes place
subtitle: Battle in Tunisia campaign
fact: The Battle of Kasserine Pass was a series of engagements which took place from 19–24 February 1943 around Kasserine Pass, a 2-mile-wide (3.2 km) gap in the Grand Dorsal chain of the Atlas Mountains in west central Tunisia.

title: Battle of Ain Jalut takes place
subtitle: Battle in Mongol invasions of the Levant
fact: The Battle of Ain Jalut, also spelled Ayn Jalut, was fought between the Bahri Mamluks of Egypt and the Ilkhanate on 3 September 1260 near the spring of Ain Jalut in southeastern Galilee in the Jezreel Valley.

title: Battle of the Hydaspes takes place
subtitle: Battle in Indian campaign of Alexander the Great
fact: The Battle of the Hydaspes also known as Battle of Jhelum, or First Battle of Jhelum, was fought between the Macedonian Empire under Alexander the Great and the Pauravas under Porus in May of 326 BC.

title: Battle of Kadesh takes place
subtitle: Battle
fact: The Battle of Kadesh was a major military conflict in the early 13th century BC, between the attacking Ramesses II of the Egyptian Empire and defending Muwatalli II of the Hittite Empire.

title: Battle of the River Plate takes place
subtitle: Battle in American Theater
fact: The Battle of the River Plate was fought in the South Atlantic on 13 December 1939 as the first British naval battle of the Second World War.

title: Allied invasion of Italy takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War II
fact: The Allied invasion of Italy was the Allied amphibious landing on mainland Italy that took place from 3 September 1943, during the Italian campaign of World War II.

title: Battle of Panipat takes place
subtitle: Battle in Mughal Empire
fact: The First Battle of Panipat, on 21 April 1526 was fought between the invading forces of Babur against Ibrahim Khan Lodi, the Sultan of Delhi, in North India.

title: Battle of Fredericksburg takes place
subtitle: Battle in American Civil War
fact: The Battle of Fredericksburg was fought December 11–15, 1862, in and around Fredericksburg, Virginia, in the Eastern Theater of the American Civil War.

title: Attack on Mers-El-Kebir takes place
subtitle: Battle in Operation Catapult
fact: The attack on Mers-el-Kébir on 3 July 1940, during the Second World War, was a British naval attack on French Navy ships at the naval base at Mers El Kébir, near Oran, on the coast of French Algeria.

title: Battle of Manila takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War II
fact: The Battle of Manila was a major battle during the Philippine campaign of 1944–45, during the Second World War.

title: Battle of the Maureaco Fields takes place
subtitle: Battle in fall of the western Roman Empire
fact: The Battle of the Catalaunian Plains, also called the Battle of the Campus Mauriacus, Battle of Châlons, Battle of Troyes or the Battle of Maurica, took place on 20 June 451 AD, between a coalition, led by the Roman general Flavius Aetius and the Visigothic king Theodoric I, against the Huns and their vassals, commanded by their king, Attila.

title: Battle of the Nile takes place
subtitle: Battle in French invasion of Egypt and Syria
fact: The Battle of the Nile was fought between the Royal Navy and the French Navy at Aboukir Bay in Egypt between 1 and 3 August 1798.

title: Battle of Poitiers takes place
subtitle: Battle in Hundred Years' War
fact: The Battle of Poitiers was fought on 19 September 1356, during the Hundred Years' War, between a French army commanded by King John II and an Anglo-Gascon force under Edward, the Black Prince.

title: Battle of Stirling Bridge takes place
subtitle: Battle in Wars of Scottish Independence
fact: The Battle of Stirling Bridge was fought during the First War of Scottish Independence.

title: Battle of Long Island takes place
subtitle: Battle in American Revolutionary War
fact: The Battle of Long Island, also known as the Battle of Brooklyn and the Battle of Brooklyn Heights, was an action of the American Revolutionary War fought on August 27, 1776, at and near the western edge of Long Island in present-day Brooklyn.

title: Battle of the Plains of Abraham takes place
subtitle: Battle in Seven Years' War
fact: The Battle of the Plains of Abraham, also known as the First Battle of Quebec, was a pivotal battle in the Seven Years' War – referred to by Americans as the French and Indian War in describing the North American theatre.

title: Battle of Trenton takes place
subtitle: Battle in American Revolutionary War
fact: The Battle of Trenton was a small but pivotal American Revolutionary War battle on the morning of December 26, 1776, in Trenton, New Jersey.

title: Battle of Blenheim takes place
subtitle: Battle in War of the Spanish Succession
fact: The Battle of Blenheim fought on 13 August [O.S.

title: Battle of Kiev takes place
subtitle: Battle in Operation Barbarossa
fact: The First Battle of Kiev or Kiev operation, known as the Battle of Kiev on the German side, was a major battle that resulted in an encirclement of Soviet troops in the vicinity of Kiev during World War II, the capital and most populous city of the Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic.

title: Battle of Issus takes place
subtitle: Battle in Wars of Alexander the Great
fact: The Battle of Issus occurred in southern Anatolia, on 5 November 333 BC between the Hellenic League led by Alexander the Great and the Achaemenid Empire, led by Darius III.

title: Battle of the Milvian Bridge takes place
subtitle: Battle in Civil Wars of the Tetrarchy
fact: The Battle of the Milvian Bridge took place between the Roman Emperors Constantine I and Maxentius on 28 October AD 312.

title: Battle of Pharsalus takes place
subtitle: Battle in Caesar's Civil War
fact: The Battle of Pharsalus was the decisive battle of Caesar's Civil War fought on 9 August 48 BC near Pharsalus in Central Greece.

title: Battle of Balaclava takes place
subtitle: Battle in Crimean War
fact: The Battle of Balaclava, fought on 25 October 1854 during the Crimean War, was part of the Siege of Sevastopol (1854–55), an Allied attempt to capture the port and fortress of Sevastopol, Russia's principal naval base on the Black Sea.

title: Siege of Budapest takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War II
fact: The Siege of Budapest or Battle of Budapest was the 50-day-long encirclement by Soviet and Romanian forces of the Hungarian capital of Budapest, near the end of World War II.

title: Battle of Chaldiran takes place
subtitle: Battle in Ottoman–Persian wars
fact: The Battle of Chaldiran took place on 23 August 1514 and ended with a decisive victory for the Ottoman Empire over the Safavid Empire.

title: Battle of the Denmark Strait takes place
subtitle: Battle in Battle of the Atlantic
fact: The Battle of the Denmark Strait was a naval engagement in the Second World War, which took place on 24 May 1941 between ships of the Royal Navy and the Kriegsmarine.

title: Battle of Ankara takes place
subtitle: Battle in Timurid invasion of the Ottoman Empire
fact: The Battle of Ankara or Angora was fought on 28 July 1402, at the Çubuk plain near Ankara, between the forces of the Ottoman sultan Bayezid I and the emir of the Timurid Empire, Timur.

title: Battle of Poltava takes place
subtitle: Battle in Great Northern War
fact: The Battle of Poltava fought on 8 July 1709, was the decisive and largest battle of the Great Northern War.

title: Battle of Kohima takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War II
fact: The Battle of Kohima was the turning point of the Japanese U-Go offensive into British India in 1944 during the Second World War.

title: Battle of Red Cliffs takes place
subtitle: Battle in Three Kingdoms
fact: The Battle of Red Cliffs, also known as the Battle of Chibi, was a decisive naval battle in China that took place during the winter of 208–209 AD.

title: Battle of Marengo takes place
subtitle: Battle in Napoleonic Wars
fact: The Battle of Marengo was fought on 14 June 1800 between French forces under the First Consul Napoleon Bonaparte and Austrian forces near the city of Alessandria, in Piedmont, Italy.

title: German invasion of the Netherlands takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War II
fact: The German invasion of the Netherlands, otherwise known as the Battle of the Netherlands, was a military campaign, part of Case Yellow, the Nazi German invasion of the Low Countries and France during World War II.

title: Battle of Jena–Auerstedt takes place
subtitle: Battle in Napoleonic Wars
fact: The twin battles of Jena and Auerstedt were fought on 14 October 1806 on the plateau west of the river Saale in today's Germany, more specifically in today's Thuringia, between the forces of Napoleon I of France and Frederick William III of Prussia, at the outset of the War of the Fourth Coalition during the Napoleonic Wars.

title: Battle of the Falkland Islands takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War I
fact: The Battle of the Falkland Islands was a First World War naval action between the British Royal Navy and Imperial German Navy on 8 December 1914 in the South Atlantic.

title: Second Battle of Bull Run takes place
subtitle: Battle in Eastern theater of the American Civil War
fact: The Second Battle of Bull Run or Battle of Second Manassas was fought August 28–30, 1862, in Prince William County, Virginia, as part of the American Civil War.

title: Battle of Varna takes place
subtitle: Battle in Crusade of Varna
fact: The Battle of Varna took place on 10 November 1444 near Varna in what is today eastern Bulgaria.

title: Battle of Coronel takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War I
fact: The Battle of Coronel was a First World War naval battle that led to an Imperial German Navy victory over the Royal Navy on 1 November 1914, off the coast of central Chile near the city of Coronel.

title: Battle of the Ice takes place
subtitle: Battle in Northern Crusades
fact: The Battle on the Ice, also known as the Battle of Lake Peipus, took place on 5 April 1242.

title: Battle of Megiddo takes place
subtitle: Battle
fact: The Battle of Megiddo was fought between Egyptian forces under the command of Pharaoh Thutmose III and a large rebellious coalition of Canaanite vassal states led by the king of Kadesh.

title: Capture of Chernobyl takes place
subtitle: Battle in full-scale Russo-Ukrainian war
fact: During the 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine, the Chernobyl Exclusion Zone was captured on 24 February, the first day of the invasion, by the Russian Armed Forces, who entered Ukrainian territory from neighbouring Belarus and seized the entire area of the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant by the end of that day.

title: Battle of Navarino takes place
subtitle: Battle in Greek War of Independence
fact: The Battle of Navarino was a naval battle fought on 20 October 1827, during the Greek War of Independence (1821–1829), in Navarino Bay, on the west coast of the Peloponnese peninsula, in the Ionian Sea.

title: Battle of Kulikovo takes place
subtitle: Battle in Mongol invasion of Kievan Rus'
fact: The Battle of Kulikovo was fought between the forces of Mamai, a powerful Mongol military commander of the Golden Horde, and Russian forces led by Grand Prince Dmitry of Moscow.

title: Battle of Guam takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War II
fact: The Battle of Guam was the American recapture of the Japanese-held island of Guam, an American territory in the Mariana Islands captured by the Japanese in the First Battle of Guam in 1941 during the Pacific campaign of World War II.

title: Battle of the Granicus takes place
subtitle: Battle in Wars of Alexander the Great
fact: The Battle of the Granicus in May 334 BC was the first of three major battles fought between Alexander the Great of Macedon and the Persian Achaemenid Empire.

title: Operation Ten-Go takes place
subtitle: Battle in Pacific War
fact: Operation Kikusui I, literally Operation Chrysanthemum Water 1, best known as Operation Ten-Go, literally Operation Heaven, was the last major Japanese naval operation in the Pacific Theater of World War II.

title: Battle of the Java Sea takes place
subtitle: Battle in Dutch East Indies campaign
fact: The Battle of the Java Sea was a decisive naval battle of the Pacific campaign of World War II.

title: Battle of Warsaw takes place
subtitle: Battle in Polish–Soviet War
fact: The Battle of Warsaw, also known as the Miracle on the Vistula, was a series of battles that resulted in a decisive Polish victory and complete disintegration of the Red Army in August 1920 during the Polish–Soviet War.

title: Battle of Valmy takes place
subtitle: Battle in War of the First Coalition
fact: The Battle of Valmy, also known as the Cannonade of Valmy, was the first major victory by the army of France during the Revolutionary Wars that followed the French Revolution.

title: Battle of Lake Trasimene takes place
subtitle: Battle in Second Punic War
fact: The Battle of Lake Trasimene was fought when a Carthaginian force under Hannibal ambushed a Roman army commanded by Gaius Flaminius on 21 June 217 BC, during the Second Punic War.

title: Battle of the Frontiers takes place
subtitle: Battle in Western Front
fact: The Battle of the Frontiers comprised battles fought along the eastern frontier of France and in southern Belgium, shortly after the outbreak of the First World War.

title: Battle of Hunayn takes place
subtitle: Battle in battles of Muhammad
fact: The Battle of Hunayn was a conflict between the Muslims of the Islamic prophet Muhammad and the tribe of Qays in the aftermath of the conquest of Mecca.

title: Battle of Falkirk takes place
subtitle: Battle in Wars of Scottish Independence
fact: The Battle of Falkirk, on 22 July 1298, was one of the major battles in the First War of Scottish Independence.

title: Battle of Guadalete takes place
subtitle: Battle in Umayyad conquest of Hispania
fact: The Battle of Guadalete was the first major battle of the Muslim conquest of the Iberian Peninsula, fought in July 711 at an unidentified location in what is now southern Spain between the Visigoths under their king, Roderic, and the invading forces of the Umayyad Caliphate, composed of mainly Berbers and some Arabs under the commander Tariq ibn Ziyad.

title: Battle of Leuctra takes place
subtitle: Battle in Boeotian War
fact: The Battle of Leuctra was fought on 6 July 371 BC between the Boeotians led by the Thebans, and the Spartans along with their allies amidst the post–Corinthian War conflict.

title: Battle of Solferino takes place
subtitle: Battle in Second Italian War of Independence
fact: The Battle of Solferino on 24 June 1859 resulted in the victory of the allied French army under Napoleon III and the Piedmont-Sardinian army under Victor Emmanuel II against the Austrian army under Emperor Franz Joseph I.

title: Battle of Puebla takes place
subtitle: Battle in Second French intervention in Mexico
fact: The Battle of Puebla, also known as the Battle of the Fifth of May took place on 5 May 1862, near Puebla de los Ángeles, during the second French intervention in Mexico.

title: Battle of Panipat takes place
subtitle: Battle
fact: The Second Battle of Panipat was fought on 5 November 1556, between the Mughals under Akbar and king Hemu, titularly known as Hemchandra Vikramaditya.

title: Battle of Legnica takes place
subtitle: Battle in Mongol invasions and conquests
fact: The Battle of Legnica, also known as the Battle of Liegnitz or Battle of Wahlstatt, was fought between the Mongol Empire and combined European forces at the village of Legnickie Pole (Wahlstatt), approximately 10 kilometres (6 mi) southeast of the city of Legnica in the Duchy of Silesia on 9 April 1241.

title: Battle of Covadonga takes place
subtitle: Battle in Reconquista
fact: The Battle of Covadonga took place in 722 between the army of Pelagius of Asturias and the army of Umayyad Caliphate commanders Alqama and Munuza.

title: Battle of Castillon takes place
subtitle: Battle in Hundred Years' War
fact: The Battle of Castillon was a battle between the forces of England and France which took place on 17 July 1453 in Gascony near the town of Castillon-sur-Dordogne.

title: Battle of the Pyramids takes place
subtitle: Battle in Napoleonic Wars
fact: The Battle of the Pyramids, also known as the Battle of Embabeh, was fought on 21 July 1798 during the French invasion of Egypt and Syria.

title: Siege of Sevastopol takes place
subtitle: Battle in Crimean War
fact: The Siege of Sevastopol lasted from October 1854 until September 1855, during the Crimean War.

title: Battle of the Allia takes place
subtitle: Battle in Roman–Gallic Wars
fact: The Battle of the Allia was fought c.

title: Battle of Smolensk takes place
subtitle: Battle in Operation Barbarossa
fact: The first Battle of Smolensk was a battle during the second phase of Operation Barbarossa, the Axis invasion of the Soviet Union, in World War II.

title: Battle of Prokhorovka takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War II
fact: The Battle of Prokhorovka was fought on 12 July 1943 near Prokhorovka, 87 kilometres (54 mi) southeast of Kursk, in the Soviet Union, during the Second World War.

title: Battle of Edessa takes place
subtitle: Battle in Shapur I's Syrio-Mesopotamian campaign
fact: The Battle of Edessa took place between the armies of the Roman Empire, under the command of Emperor Valerian, and the Sasanian Empire under Shapur I, in Edessa in 260.

title: Battle of Chaeronea takes place
subtitle: Battle in Fourth Sacred War
fact: The Battle of Chaereonea was fought in 338 BC, near the city of Chaeronea in Boeotia, between Macedonia under Philip II and an alliance of city-states led by Athens and Thebes.

title: Battle of Arsuf takes place
subtitle: Battle in Third Crusade
fact: The Battle of Arsuf took place on 7 September 1191, as part of the Third Crusade.

title: Battle of Mohi takes place
subtitle: Battle in Mongol invasions and conquests
fact: The Battle of Mohi was a pivotal conflict between the Mongol Empire and the Kingdom of Hungary during the Mongol invasion of Europe.

title: Battle of Friedland takes place
subtitle: Battle in Napoleonic Wars
fact: The Battle of Friedland was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars between the armies of the French Empire and the Russian Empire led by Napoleon I and General Levin August von Bennigsen respectively.

title: Battle of Königgrätz takes place
subtitle: Battle in Austro-Prussian War
fact: The Battle of Königgrätz was the decisive battle of the Austro-Prussian War in which the Kingdom of Prussia defeated the Austrian Empire.

title: Battle of Vittorio Veneto takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War I
fact: The Battle of Vittorio Veneto was fought from 24 October to 3 November 1918 near Vittorio Veneto on the Italian Front during World War I.

title: Battle of Alcácer Quibir takes place
subtitle: Battle in Moroccan–Portuguese conflicts
fact: The Battle of Alcácer Quibir was fought in northern Morocco, near the town of Ksar-el-Kebir and Larache, on 4 August 1578.

title: Battle of Zenta takes place
subtitle: Battle in Great Turkish War
fact: The Battle of Zenta, also known as the Battle of Senta, took place on 11 September 1697 near Zenta, in the Kingdom of Hungary, then under Ottoman occupation.

title: Battle of Nagashino takes place
subtitle: Battle
fact: The Battle of Nagashino was a famous battle in Japanese history, fought in 1575 at Nagashino in Mikawa Province.

title: Battle of White Mountain takes place
subtitle: Battle in Bohemian Revolt
fact: The Battle of White Mountain was fought on 8 November 1620 outside Prague in the early stages of the Thirty Years' War.

title: Battle of the Golden Spurs takes place
subtitle: Battle in Franco-Flemish War
fact: The Battle of the Golden Spurs or 1302 Battle of Courtrai was a military confrontation between the royal army of France and rebellious forces of the County of Flanders on 11 July 1302 during the 1297–1305 Franco-Flemish War.

title: Battle of Nahāvand takes place
subtitle: Battle in Muslim conquest of Persia
fact: The Battle of Nahavand, also spelled Nihavand or Nahawand, was fought in 642 between the Rashidun Muslim forces under An-Numan ibn Muqarrin and Sasanian army under King Yazdegerd III.

title: Battle of the Kalka River takes place
subtitle: Battle in Mongol invasions and conquests
fact: The Battle of the Kalka River was fought between the Mongol Empire, whose armies were led by Jebe and Subutai, and a coalition of several Rus' principalities, including Kiev and Galicia-Volhynia, and the Cumans under Köten.

title: Battle of Maritsa takes place
subtitle: Battle in Serbian–Ottoman Wars
fact: The Battle of Maritsa, also known as the Battle of Chernomen, was fought on 26 September 1371 near the Maritsa River, close to the village of Chernomen.

title: Battle of Munda takes place
subtitle: Battle in Caesar's Civil War
fact: The Battle of Munda, in southern Hispania Ulterior, was the final battle of Caesar's civil war against the leaders of the Optimates.

title: Battle of Breitenfeld takes place
subtitle: Battle in Thirty Years' War
fact: The Battle of Breitenfeld took place during the Thirty Years' War on 17 September 1631 near Breitenfeld, Leipzig, in modern Saxony.

title: Battle of the Trebia takes place
subtitle: Battle in Second Punic War
fact: The Battle of the Trebia was the first major battle of the Second Punic War, fought between the Carthaginian forces of Hannibal and a Roman army under Sempronius Longus on 22 or 23 December 218 BC.

title: Battle of Yamen takes place
subtitle: Battle in Mongol conquest of the Song dynasty
fact: The Battle of Yamen, also known as the Battle of Yashan, was a naval battle which took place in southern China on 19 March 1279.

title: Battle of Rocroi takes place
subtitle: Battle in Franco-Spanish War
fact: The Battle of Rocroi, fought on 19 May 1643, was a major engagement of the Thirty Years' War between a French army, led by the 21-year-old Duke of Enghien and Spanish forces under General Francisco de Melo only five days after the accession of Louis XIV to the throne of France after his father's death.

title: Siege of Przemyśl takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War I
fact: The Siege of Przemyśl was the longest siege in Europe during the First World War, and the second largest in the entire conflict, after the Siege of Medina.

title: Battle of Rossbach takes place
subtitle: Battle in Seven Years' War
fact: The Battle of Rossbach took place on 5 November 1757 during the Third Silesian War near the village of Rossbach (Roßbach), in the Electorate of Saxony.

title: Battle of Nedao takes place
subtitle: Battle
fact: The Battle of Nedao was fought in Pannonia in 454 between the Huns and their former Germanic vassals.

title: Snake Island campaign takes place
subtitle: Battle in full-scale Russo-Ukrainian war
fact: The Snake Island campaign was a period of Russian occupation and military conflict for Snake Island, a small, strategically located Ukrainian island in the Black Sea.

title: Battle of Vitoria takes place
subtitle: Battle in Peninsular War
fact: At the Battle of Vitoria, a British, Portuguese and Spanish army under the Marquess of Wellington broke the French army under King Joseph Bonaparte and Marshal Jean-Baptiste Jourdan near Vitoria in Spain, eventually leading to victory in the Peninsular War.

title: Battle of Fontenoy takes place
subtitle: Battle in War of the Austrian Succession
fact: The Battle of Fontenoy took place on 11 May 1745 during the War of the Austrian Succession, near Tournai, then in the Austrian Netherlands, now Belgium.

title: Battle of the Chesapeake takes place
subtitle: Battle in American Revolutionary War
fact: The Battle of the Chesapeake, also known as the Battle of the Virginia Capes or simply the Battle of the Capes, was a crucial naval battle in the American Revolutionary War that took place near the mouth of the Chesapeake Bay on 5 September 1781.

title: Battle of Artemisium takes place
subtitle: Battle in Greco-Persian Wars
fact: The Battle of Artemisium or Artemision was a series of naval engagements over three days during the second Persian invasion of Greece.

title: Battle of Magnesia takes place
subtitle: Battle in Roman–Seleucid War
fact: The Battle of Magnesia took place in either December 190 or January 189 BC.

title: Battle of Cape Ecnomus takes place
subtitle: Battle in First Punic War
fact: The Battle of Cape Ecnomus or Eknomos was a naval battle, fought off southern Sicily, in 256 BC, between the fleets of Carthage and the Roman Republic, during the First Punic War.

title: Battle of Narva takes place
subtitle: Battle in Great Northern War
fact: The Battle of Narva on 30 November 1700 was an early battle in the Great Northern War.

title: Battle of Białystok–Minsk takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War II
fact: The Battle of Białystok–Minsk was a German strategic operation conducted by the Wehrmacht's Army Group Center under Field Marshal Fedor von Bock during the penetration of the Soviet border region in the opening stage of Operation Barbarossa, lasting from 22 June to 9 July 1941.

title: Siege of Kobanî takes place
subtitle: Battle in Syrian Civil War
fact: The siege of Kobanî was launched by the Islamic State (IS) on 13 September 2014, in order to capture the Kobanî Canton and its main city of Kobanî in northern Syria, in the de facto autonomous region of Rojava.

title: Battle of Cynoscephalae takes place
subtitle: Battle in Second Macedonian War
fact: The Battle of Cynoscephalae was an encounter battle fought in Thessaly in 197 BC between the Roman-Aetolian army, led by Titus Quinctius Flamininus, and Antigonid Macedon, led by Philip V, during the Second Macedonian War.

title: Battle of Pydna takes place
subtitle: Battle in Third Macedonian War
fact: The Battle of Pydna took place in 168 BC between Rome and Macedon during the Third Macedonian War.

title: Battle of Preveza takes place
subtitle: Battle in Third Ottoman–Venetian War
fact: The Battle of Preveza was a naval engagement that occurred on 28 September 1538 near Preveza in the Ionian Sea in northwest Greece, in the same area as the Battle of Actium in 31 BC.

title: Battle of Lissa takes place
subtitle: Battle in Austro-Prussian War
fact: The Battle of Lissa was fought on 20 July 1866 between the Italian and Austrian navies near the island of Lissa in the Adriatic Sea during the Third Italian War of Independence.

title: Battle of Köse Dağ takes place
subtitle: Battle in Mongol conquest of Anatolia
fact: The Battle of Köse Dağ took place in eastern Anatolia on 26 June 1243 when an army of the Sultanate of Rum, led by Sultan Kaykhusraw II, confronted an invading Mongol army under the general Baiju and was decisively defeated.

title: Battle of Sluys takes place
subtitle: Battle in Hundred Years' War
fact: The Battle of Sluys, also called the Battle of l'Écluse, was a naval battle fought on 24 June 1340 between England and France.

title: Battle of Smolensk takes place
subtitle: Battle in French invasion of Russia
fact: The Battle of Smolensk was the first major battle of the French invasion of Russia.

title: Battle of Kleidion takes place
subtitle: Battle in Byzantine conquest of Bulgaria
fact: The Battle of Kleidion took place on 29 July 1014, between the Byzantine Empire and the Bulgarian Empire.

title: Battle of Mons Graupius takes place
subtitle: Battle in Roman conquest of Britain
fact: The Battle of Mons Graupius was, according to Tacitus, a Roman military victory in what is now Scotland, taking place in AD 83 or, less probably, 84.

title: Battle of Ipsus takes place
subtitle: Battle in Fourth War of the Diadochi
fact: The Battle of Ipsus was fought between some of the Diadochi in 301 BC near the town of Ipsus in Phrygia.

title: Battle of Sinop takes place
subtitle: Battle in Crimean War
fact: The Battle of Sinop, or the Battle of Sinope, was a naval battle that took place on 30 November 1853 between Imperial Russia and the Ottoman Empire during the opening phase of the Crimean War (1853–1856).

title: Battle of Chesma takes place
subtitle: Battle in Sixth Russo-Turkish War
fact: The Battle of Chesma took place on 5–7 July 1770 during the near and in Çeşme, in the area between the western tip of Anatolia and the island of Chios, which was the site of a number of past naval battles between the Ottoman Empire and the Republic of Venice.

title: Kerensky Offensive takes place
subtitle: Battle in WWI Eastern Front
fact: The Kerensky offensive, also called the summer offensive, the June offensive in Russia, or the July offensive in Western historiography, took place from 1 July [O.S.

title: Battle of Malplaquet takes place
subtitle: Battle in War of the Spanish Succession
fact: The Battle of Malplaquet took place on 11 September 1709 during the War of the Spanish Succession, near Taisnières-sur-Hon in France, then part of the Spanish Netherlands.

title: Battle of Lechfeld takes place
subtitle: Battle in Hungarian invasions of Europe
fact: The Battle of Lechfeld, also known as the Second Battle of Lechfeld, was a series of military engagements over the course of three days from 10–12 August 955 in which the Kingdom of Germany, led by King Otto I the Great, defeated the Hungarian army led by Harka Bulcsú and the chieftains Lél and Súr.

title: Battle of Lake Khasan takes place
subtitle: Battle in Soviet–Japanese border conflicts
fact: The Battle of Lake Khasan, also known as the Changkufeng Incident, was an attempted military incursion by Manchukuo, a Japanese puppet state, into territory claimed and controlled by the Soviet Union.

title: Battle of Thapsus takes place
subtitle: Battle in Caesar's Civil War
fact: The Battle of Thapsus was a military engagement that took place on April 6, 46 BC near Thapsus.

title: Battle of Ulm takes place
subtitle: Battle in Napoleonic Wars
fact: The Battle of Ulm on 16–19 October 1805 was a series of skirmishes, at the end of the Ulm Campaign, which allowed Napoleon I to trap an entire Austrian army under the command of Karl Freiherr Mack von Leiberich with minimal losses and to force its surrender near Ulm in the Electorate of Bavaria.

title: Battle of Fulford takes place
subtitle: Battle in Viking activity in the British Isles
fact: The Battle of Fulford was fought on the outskirts of the village of Fulford, just south of York in England, on 20 September 1066.

title: Black January takes place
subtitle: Battle in dissolution of the Soviet Union
fact: Black January, also known as Black Saturday or the January Massacre, was a violent crackdown on Azerbaijani nationalism and anti-Soviet sentiment in Baku on 19–20 January 1990, as part of a state of emergency during the dissolution of the Soviet Union.

title: Battle of Myriokephalon takes place
subtitle: Battle in Byzantine–Seljuq Wars
fact: The Battle of Myriokephalon was a battle between the Byzantine Empire and the Seljuk Turks in the mountains west of Iconium (Konya) in west-central Anatolia on 17 September 1176.

title: Gorlice–Tarnów Offensive takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War I
fact: The Gorlice–Tarnów offensive during World War I was initially conceived as a minor German offensive to relieve Russian pressure on the Austro-Hungarians to their south on the Eastern Front, but resulted in the Central Powers' chief offensive effort of 1915, causing the total collapse of the Russian lines and their retreat far into Russia.

title: Battle of Ayacucho takes place
subtitle: Battle in Peruvian War of Independence
fact: The Battle of Ayacucho was a decisive military encounter during the Peruvian War of Independence.

title: Battle of Cunaxa takes place
subtitle: Battle in the Ten Thousand
fact: The Battle of Cunaxa was fought in the late summer of 401 BC between the Persian king Artaxerxes II and his brother Cyrus the Younger for control of the Achaemenid throne.

title: Battle of Mycale takes place
subtitle: Battle in Greco-Persian Wars
fact: The Battle of Mycale was one of the two major battles that ended the second Persian invasion of Greece during the Greco-Persian Wars.

title: Battle of Legnano takes place
subtitle: Battle in Guelphs and Ghibellines
fact: The battle of Legnano was a battle between the imperial army of Frederick Barbarossa and the troops of the Lombard League on 29 May 1176, near the town of Legnano, in present-day Lombardy, Italy.

title: Battle of Alma takes place
subtitle: Battle in Crimean War
fact: The Battle of the Alma took place during the Crimean War between an allied expeditionary force and Russian forces defending the Crimean Peninsula on 20 September 1854.

title: Demyansk Pocket takes place
subtitle: Battle in Siege of Leningrad
fact: The Demyansk Pocket was the name given to the pocket of German troops encircled by the Red Army around Demyansk, south of Leningrad, on World War II's Eastern Front.

title: Battle of Smolensk takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War II
fact: The second Smolensk operation was a Soviet strategic offensive operation conducted by the Red Army as part of the Summer-Autumn Campaign of 1943.

title: Battle of Dorylaeum takes place
subtitle: Battle in First Crusade
fact: The Battle of Dorylaion or Dorylaeum took place during the First Crusade on 1 July 1097 between the crusader forces and the Seljuk Turks, near the city of Dorylaion in Anatolia.

title: Battle of Sagrajas takes place
subtitle: Battle in Almoravid conquest of al-Andalus
fact: The Battle of Sagrajas, also called Zalaca or Zallaqah, was a conflict fought in 1086 between the Almoravid army, led by their king, Yusuf ibn Tashfin, and the forces of King Alfonso VI of Castile.

title: Battle of Ticinus takes place
subtitle: Battle in Second Punic War
fact: The Battle of Ticinus was fought between the Carthaginian forces of Hannibal and a Roman army under Publius Cornelius Scipio in late November 218 BC as part of the Second Punic War.

title: Battle of Mohács takes place
subtitle: Battle in Great Turkish War
fact: The Second Battle of Mohács, also known as the Battle of Harsány Mountain, was fought on 12 August 1687 between the forces of Ottoman Sultan Mehmed IV, commanded by the Grand Vizier Sarı Süleyman Pasha, and the forces of Leopold I, Holy Roman Emperor, commanded by Charles of Lorraine.

title: Battle of Bapheus takes place
subtitle: Battle in Byzantine–Ottoman wars
fact: The Battle of Bapheus occurred on 27 July 1302, between an Ottoman army under Osman I and a Byzantine army under George Mouzalon.

title: Battle of Lodi takes place
subtitle: Battle in Napoleonic Wars
fact: The Battle of Lodi was fought on 10 May 1796 between French forces under Napoleon Bonaparte and an Austrian rear guard led by Karl Philipp Sebottendorf at Lodi, Lombardy.

title: Battle of Vouille takes place
subtitle: Battle in Franco-Visigothic Wars
fact: The Battle of Vouillé was a battle in the Franco-Gothic War and was fought in the northern marches of Visigothic territory, at Vouillé, near Poitiers (Gaul), around spring 507 between the Franks, commanded by Clovis, and the Visigoths, commanded by Alaric II.

title: Battle of Heraclea takes place
subtitle: Battle in Pyrrhic War
fact: The Battle of Heraclea took place in 280 BC between the Romans under the command of consul Publius Valerius Laevinus, and the combined forces of Greeks from Epirus, Tarentum, Thurii, Metapontum, and Heraclea under the command of Pyrrhus, king of Epirus.

title: Battle of Sievierodonetsk takes place
subtitle: Battle in Russo-Ukrainian War
fact: The Battle of Sievierodonetsk was a military engagement in the wider battle of Donbas of the eastern Ukraine campaign during the Russian invasion of Ukraine.

title: Battle of Avarayr takes place
subtitle: Battle in War of the Vardanants
fact: The Battle of Avarayr was fought on 26 May 451 on the Avarayr Plain in Vaspurakan between a Christian Armenian army under Vardan Mamikonian and Sassanid Persia.

title: Battle of Svolder takes place
subtitle: Battle in Viking Age Norway
fact: The Battle of Svolder was a large naval battle during the Viking age, fought in September 1000 in the western Baltic Sea between King Olaf of Norway and an alliance of the Kings of Denmark and Sweden and Olaf's enemies in Norway.

title: Battle of Rostov takes place
subtitle: Battle in World War II
fact: The Battle of Rostov (1941) took place on the Eastern Front of World War II around Rostov-on-Don and was fought between Army Group South of Nazi Germany and the Southern Front of the Soviet Union.

title: Battle of the Neva takes place
subtitle: Battle in Swedish–Novgorodian Wars
fact: The Battle of the Neva was fought between the Novgorod Republic, along with Karelians, and the Kingdom of Sweden, including Norwegian, Finnish and Tavastian forces, on the Neva River, near the settlement of Ust-Izhora, on 15 July 1240.

title: Battle of Saule takes place
subtitle: Battle in Livonian Crusade
fact: The Battle of Saule was fought on 22 September 1236, between the Livonian Brothers of the Sword and pagan troops of Samogitians and Semigallians.

title: Battle of Gangut takes place
subtitle: Battle in Great Northern War
fact: The Battle of Gangut took place on 27 JulyJul.
